transcript: Speaker A: You can understand English, but when you try to speak, the words just don't come. Does that sound like you? Here's why that happens. You've been learning English passively for years, taking information in—videos, podcasts, movies—but almost never putting it out. And so, when it's time to actually speak, your brain doesn't know what to do. Not because you don't know enough words, but because you've never practiced producing them under pressure. The fix is simple, but it has to be active. You need to build sentences out loud, get corrected in real time, and keep going before fear has a chance to stop you. That's exactly what happens in every conversation with me. You speak, I respond. When you make a mistake—and you will, and that's good—I catch it immediately, and we move on. You already know enough English to start. You just need to start producing it. Talk to me today.
on_screen_text: you can understand English but when you try to speak the words just don't come does that sound like you here's why that happens you've been learning English passively for years taking information in videos podcast movies but almost never putting it out and so when it's time to actually speak your brain doesn't know what to do not because you don't know enough words but because you've never practiced producing them under pressure the fix is simple but it has to be active you need to build sentences out loud get corrected in real time and keep going before fear has a chance to stop you that's exactly what happens in every conversation with me you speak I respond when you make a mistake and you will and that's good I catch it immediately and we move on you already know enough English to start you just need to start producing it talk to me today
summary: An AI avatar explains that passive learning prevents people from speaking English fluently. She introduces an interactive AI tool that offers real-time corrections to help users practice active speaking. The call to action encourages users to start practicing immediately.
